seoTitle: High Range vs Highfields — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between High Range and Highfields. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: High Range vs Highfields: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in High Range and Highfields.
heading: High Range vs Highfields — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between High Range, New South Wales and Highfields, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: High Range has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Highfields (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is High Range or Highfields safer from flooding?
faq.answer: High Range has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Highfields scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: High Range scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Highfields scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in High Range or Highfields?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. High Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
